zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose於1194年之前與Ralph de Braose在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose于1194年之前与Ralph de Braose在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose married Ralph de Braose before 1194, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose a épousé Ralph de Braose avant 1194, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose hat vor 1194 in England Ralph de Braose geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose lei sposato Ralph de Braose prima del 1194, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braoseは1194年以前にイギリスでRalph de Braoseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose(은)는 1194년 이전 영국에서 Ralph de Braose(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose casou-se com Ralph de Braose antes 1194, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph de Braose перед 1194 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Ralph de Braose se casó con Ralph de Braose antes de 1194, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
